subject: rpmsg: qcom_glink_native: fix error return code of qcom_glink_rx_data()
notes: Independent POC generated for rep2 (not reused).
Strict verifier pass on first POC attempt: F1.acq=0 F1.rel=0 F3.goto=2 F5.ret=2 F2.if=3 (using poc-for-verifier.c, a buggy-only slice, to align counts with seed-slice).

Initial query required a negative-assignment to exist in the function as a sanity filter; that filter wrongly excluded _buggy because the bug is precisely the absence of any negative assignment. Repaired in 1 iter by removing the gating predicate and instead requiring NO negative assignment between the log call and the return.

Final query (~95 LOC) is generic over the kernel: matches any function declaring int ret/err/rc/error/status = 0, an error-log call (dev_err/pr_err/netdev_err/WARN*), a goto whose label-target reaches `return v;`, with no negative assignment to v between log and return. No POC artifact names hard-coded.
POC sha256 frozen at 5a4501a4f55... and verified unchanged after Stage F.
